evals.prompt: hey can you make a gif of my last coding session? I was working on the auth middleware earlier today
evals.expected_output: Should check for uvx, search for sessions matching 'auth middleware', present matches for confirmation, then generate a GIF with OS-appropriate chrome style and --open flag

evals.prompt: I want to create an animated replay of a session but I want to customize how it looks — dark theme, show the tool usage, linux window frame
evals.expected_output: Should walk through options, use --chrome linux, suggest dark color schemes, use --show tools, and construct the right uvx command

evals.prompt: show me my recent Claude Code sessions so I can pick one to turn into a gif
evals.expected_output: Should list recent sessions from ~/.claude/projects/ with summaries and dates in a numbered picker format, then generate after user picks

evals.prompt: make an mp4 of the session at tests/sample_session.jsonl with the Catppuccin Mocha color scheme
evals.expected_output: Should use uvx agent-log-gif json tests/sample_session.jsonl -o <name>.mp4 --format mp4 --color-scheme 'Catppuccin Mocha' --open, checking for ffmpeg first
Should handle Codex session file correctly — use uvx agent-log-gif json with the file path, detect it's Codex format automatically
